title: From Validity to Inter-Subjectivity: An Argument for Reliability Signals in Search Environments

section_anchors.text: Search engines and information platforms are increasingly scrutinized for their role in spreading misinformation
Since this makes validity hard to judge in practice, I argue that we should shift attention to the reliability of information
If reliability is to meaningfully guide information evaluation, it assumes that people expect others to agree on what constitutes good information
Drawing on practice theory, it shows that users do not treat judgments as subjective, but appeal to shared standards grounded in the norms of specific practices
We propose knowledge context (Smith and Rieh, 2019) as a way for information systems to support better judgments about reliability
Most current systems hide this kind of context
Platforms are often caught between two extremes: suppressing freedom of speech in the name of truth, or claiming neutrality while amplifying unreliable content
A focus on reliability, and the social cues that shape it, offers a path toward more transparent, democratic, and epistemically responsible information environments
